summary: The tongue primarily forms from parts derived from multiple pharyngeal arches; however, arytenoid swellings are an exception as they develop separately contributing minimally if at all directly towards main tongue formation. Thus, option D correctly identifies what does NOT form part of a standard tongue’s primary tissue mass during embryonic growth stages based upon detailed studies outlined here regarding human orofacial morphogenesis processes involving these critical early-stage cell clusters differentiating into distinct oral cavity features including but beyond merely tongues themselves extending even further down respiratory tracts like vocal cords etc., yet still maintaining their unique origins outside those typically involved when discussing 'the' tongue itself strictly speaking without broader
